input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-04T00:57:17.036Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension on any property:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Each `AppThemeBinding` takes a `Light` and `Dark` value. MAUI resolves the correct one automatically based on the OS theme and updates live if the user toggles it.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` extension method:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme<Color>(Label.TextColorProperty, 
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Tip — use resource dictionaries** to avoid scattering hex codes everywhere:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">
    <AppThemeBinding Light="#FFFFFF" Dark="#1E1E1E" />
</Color>

<!-- Then reference it -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```

No event subscriptions or manual detection needed — `AppThemeBinding` reacts to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` internally.
